page: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dldsv7 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most standard workloads, with notable strengths in specific single-threaded operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is 2267.08, and the multi-core score is 9229.96. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier levels at 35.65 GB/sec and 14.82 GB/sec respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 650.22 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 86.93 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In language model inference tests using a small 135M model, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 267.16 tokens/sec, while prompt processing averages 999.12 tokens/sec.

The D8lds_v7 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades memory density for compute power and local storage capacity. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of a 472 GB local disk provides fast local scratch space, though the lack of bundled public IPv4 addresses is a configuration detail to note. This server is suited for tasks such as single-threaded data compression, lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Dldsv7 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, providing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 472 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers average multi-core performance but excels in single-threaded tasks, achieving top-tier results in gzip compression (35.65 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.82 GB/sec). It also demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 267.16 tokens/sec for small LLM models. With its low-memory profile and local disk capacity, the D8lds_v7 is a cost-efficient choice for compute-intensive workloads, lightweight web serving, and data compression tasks that do not demand high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v7 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, 16 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage, designed for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v7 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized server with an Intel Xeon processor, 16 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, dedicated vCPUs, and local storage for memory-lean workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Includes 472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks
Suited for compute-intensive, low-memory workloads and lightweight web serving
